summary: Amara is a 22-year-old self-taught phone repair technician from outside Accra who has run her own stall since age 17. She speaks Twi, Ga, and English, taught herself JavaScript from YouTube, and manages her own inventory and supplier relationships.

skills.evidence: Running phone repair business since age 17

skills.source_activity: Diagnosing which phone component is faulty
Ordering parts before they run out

intake_transcript.question: What do you spend most of your time doing each day?
intake_transcript.answer: I repair phones at my stall near Accra. I buy broken phones, fix them and sell them, or fix phones for customers.

intake_transcript.question: What skills or things have you learned on your own?
intake_transcript.answer: I taught myself coding from YouTube. I watch videos on JavaScript when I have free time at the stall.

intake_transcript.question: What languages do you speak?
intake_transcript.answer: I speak Twi, Ga, and English. I use English and Twi with customers.

intake_transcript.question: Have you ever taught someone or organized people?
intake_transcript.answer: Yes, I taught my younger brother how to replace phone screens. I also helped two girls from my neighborhood learn how to use smartphones.

intake_transcript.question: What tools or technology do you use regularly?
intake_transcript.answer: Soldering iron, screwdrivers, multimeter for testing circuits. I also use WhatsApp to contact my parts suppliers.
